Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate 3B/S3. Write /tmp/threeb_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/threeb_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Plan V4 3B: flakiness policy BEFORE CI becomes required — N-run stability, quarantine flaky NON-governing tests (governing RACE/faithfulness NEVER quarantined), define the bar, add dep-hash+license+secret scanning, keep CI report-only until provably stable. This is the ONLY pytest that actually executes in CI. python_ci.yml (report-only) runs no pytest — only ruff/mypy/interrogate, all with `|| true` under a job-level `continue-on-error: true`. Local baseline collection uses: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q","governing_tests":["tests/oracle/test_cassette.py (oracle acceptance / deterministic record-replay cassette)","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_001_two_family_segregation.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_002_provenance_tokens.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_003_strict_verify.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_004_zero_verified_abort.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_005_corpus_approval.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_006_budget_imputation.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_007_delimiter_sanitization.py","tests/crown_jewels/test_cj_008_entailment_correctness.py","tests/polaris_graph/test_fx02_strict_verify_floors_iready017.py","tests/polaris_graph/test_l4_multilingual_strict_verify.py","tests/polaris_graph/test_strict_verify_cleaned_text_integration.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify_entailment.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify_entailment_live.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify_qualifier_retention.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify_telemetry.py","tests/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/test_strict_verify_unknown_mode_warning.py","tests/dr_benchmark/test_lane_b_gen_faithfulness_completeness_beatboth5.py","tests/polaris_graph/test_f07_f04_faithfulness_resume_iarch004.py","tests/polaris_graph/generator/test_breadth_corroborator_faithfulness_iarch007.py","tests/v6/acceptance/test_dramatiq_acceptance.py (RACE/broker acceptance — currently excluded from CI via --ignore=tests/v6/acceptance; needs a live broker)","tests/v6/acceptance/test_runs_db_integration.py (acceptance — live DB)"],"baseline":"16738 tests collected, 11 collection errors (matches expected 16738/11). Collection was Interrupted by the 11 errors; e.g. ERROR tests/unit/test_perspective_tracking.py. Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --collect-only -q (from /home/polaris/wt/phase-3b, branch chore/review-readiness-3b off chore/review-readiness-phase1). Worktree created at /home/polaris/wt/phase-3b, HEAD c5448ec 'Phase 1C docs: record migration status + 20 conflicting-default findings'."}, flakiness={"flaky_tests":[],"consistent_failures":[],"run_pass_counts":"INCONCLUSIVE — the 3x serial sweep did not finish within the available time. Run 1 was still executing at ~70% when output was forced; runs 2 and 3 had not started. No run produced a completed pass/fail summary or JUnit XML (pytest emits the -rf FAILED node-id list and --junit-xml only at session end), so no per-run pass counts are available yet. Earlier apparent completions ("9256 passed", "RUN1 END rc=1") were confirmed PHANTOM background-task notifications — verified against ground truth (pgrep + serial.log + xml presence), the referenced processes were still running and no xml existed. A single serial run over this subset takes ~11-13+ min wall (the tests/polaris_graph ML tail — retrieval ~221, synthesis ~270, clinical_retrieval ~122, llm ~104 test funcs doing real CPU embedding/NLI inference — is a slow band that plateaued at ~68-70% for several minutes while confirmed CPU-active, not hung: ~7-8 cores busy, 326 threads, state R).","notes":"SCOPE/METHOD: Ran `/home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit tests/polaris_graph -p no:cacheprovider -p no:randomly -m 'not slow and not api and not live' --continue-on-collection-errors --junit-xml=runN.xml -rf -q` STRICTLY SERIALLY via a driver at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh (with a guard refusing to start if another pytest is running). tests/oracle was excluded per instructions (not in the selected paths). Markers present in pytest.ini: slow, api, live (no separate 'integration' marker). Subset selects ~9455 tests (5 deselected by markers).

CRITICAL PROCESS FINDING — CONCURRENCY CONTAMINATION: My first two runs accidentally overlapped (run 1 was still alive when I launched run 2). Two pytest sessions sharing filesystem/temp/port/model state produced a burst of ~90 early failures (FFFF clustered at 0-2%, in early-alphabetical polaris_graph dirs). These are ARTIFACTS of parallel execution, NOT real flakiness. I killed the contaminated run 2 and restarted everything strictly serial. Any future characterization MUST serialize — the suite is not safe to run concurrently with itself.

CRITICAL ENV FINDING — UNRELIABLE BACKGROUND NOTIFICATIONS: This environment's background-task/monitor completion notifications repeatedly fired FALSE/future events (e.g. 'RUN1 END rc=1 16:24:19' delivered while the wall clock was 16:14 and the process was still running; a phantom '9256 passed' summary). Ground truth must be taken ONLY from pgrep -f 'runN.xml', the driver's serial.log markers, and presence of runN.xml. Do not trust the notification text.

COLLECTION ERRORS — 35 (NOT 11): The task mentioned 11 known pre-existing collection errors; this worktree/branch (chore/review-readiness-3b) actually shows 35 collection ImportErrors in this subset (verified via --co). These are separate from flaky tests. By directory: audit_bundle (10), api (8), benchmark (8), unit (6: test_clarification_agent, test_hle_benchmark, test_mesh_api, test_mesh_cli, test_mesh_snapshot, test_perspective_tracking), plus audit_ir/test_manifest_augment, anti_sycophancy/test_stance_delta, and top-level test_demo_smoke. Full list captured. The count discrepancy (35 vs 11) is itself worth flagging — the recent config migration on this branch (commits: 'migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()', 'central defaults registry') is a plausible source of new import/env breakage and should be investigated.

DELIVERABLE STATUS: flaky_tests=[] and consistent_failures=[] are returned EMPTY because I have NO completed multi-run data to substantiate either — reporting node-ids now would be fabrication. To complete this correctly, let the serial driver (still running; serial.log + trace.log + run1/2/3.xml under the scratchpad flaky/ dir) finish all 3 runs, then diff the three run*.xml files: node-ids failing in some-but-not-all runs = flaky; failing in all 3 = consistent non-governing failures. NOTE: the early-suite failures seen under contamination need re-confirmation under clean serial conditions before being classified — under load they were parallel-execution artifacts, but some may reproduce serially (potential real regressions from the config migration), which the completed run1.xml will reveal.

ARTIFACTS: driver=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/serial_runs.sh; live logs+xml=/tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/flaky/ (serial.log, trace.log, run1.log/run1.xml, etc.). No governing tests were quarantined or modified (characterization only)."}, policy={"files_written":["/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b/docs/review_readiness/flakiness_policy.md","/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b/docs/flaky_quarantine.txt","/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b/docs/review_readiness/ci_scanning.md","/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b/.github/workflows/python_ci.yml","/home/polaris/wt/phase-3b/pytest.ini"],"quarantine_count":0,"scanning_added":["pip check (dependency integrity, report-only)","pip-audit (dependency vulnerability/CVE scan, report-only)","pip-licenses (license scan, report-only)","detect-secrets (secret scan, report-only)"],"ci_still_report_only":true}.
Ask codex: "Does this establish a sound flakiness policy that (a) protects the governing measurement from ever being quarantined, (b) tracks flaky non-governing tests without deleting them, (c) adds dep-hash/license/secret scanning as report-only, and (d) keeps CI report-only (not flipped to required)? Any fabricated scan results or a governing test wrongly quarantined? End with 3B-OK or 3B-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll gate 3B/S3 with CODEX. Let me first write the gate prompt file, then run it.

input.content:
You are the gate reviewer for Plan V4 Phase 3B (flakiness policy + supply-chain scanning, BEFORE CI becomes required). Judge ONLY the evidence below. Do not assume anything not stated. Be adversarial: hunt for fabricated scan results, a governing test that got wrongly quarantined, or CI silently flipped to required.

# PHASE 3B INTENT
Establish a flakiness policy BEFORE CI becomes required:
- N-run stability characterization; quarantine flaky NON-governing tests only.
- Governing tests (RACE/broker acceptance + faithfulness/strict-verify + crown jewels + oracle) are NEVER quarantined.
- Define the stability bar.
- Add dependency-hash + license + secret scanning.
- Keep CI report-only until provably stable (do NOT flip to required).

# EVIDENCE

## setup
- ci_report_only: true
- existing_scanning: NONE of the required scan classes present — no dependency-hash check (no pip --require-hashes / hashed requirements), no license scan (no pip-licenses/licensecheck/cyclonedx/SBOM), no secret scan (no gitleaks/detect-secrets), no SAST/vuln scan (no bandit/pip-audit/safety/semgrep/trivy).
- Closest existing supply-chain hygiene (NOT the above): GitHub Actions SHA-pinned in every workflow; web_ci.yml 'verify_pip_resolution' job runs `pip install --dry-run -r requirements.txt` and `-r requirements-v6.txt` to catch ResolutionImpossible early.
- sha256sum usage in codex-required.yml / codex-visual-required.yml / codex_verdict_check.yml is Codex review-artifact diff-binding (hashing the PR diff/rubric/UI-surface tree), NOT dependency-hash pinning.
- test_command (the ONLY pytest that executes in CI): `pytest tests/v6/ --ignore=tests/v6/acceptance -q --strict-markers --no-header` (web_ci.yml pytest_v6_backend, PYTHONPATH=src). python_ci.yml is report-only, runs no pytest — only ruff/mypy/interrogate, all with `|| true` under job-level `continue-on-error: true`.
- governing_tests: oracle cassette (record-replay); crown_jewels cj_001..cj_008; polaris_graph strict_verify family (fx02, l4_multilingual, cleaned_text_integration, clinical_generator strict_verify x6); dr_benchmark lane_b gen faithfulness; polaris_graph faithfulness_resume iarch004; breadth_corroborator faithfulness iarch007; tests/v6/acceptance dramatiq (RACE/broker — currently excluded from CI via --ignore, needs live broker) + runs_db_integration (live DB).
- baseline: 16738 tests collected, 11 collection errors (matches expected). Worktree /home/polaris/wt/phase-3b, branch chore/review-readiness-3b off chore/review-readiness-phase1, HEAD c5448ec.

## flakiness (characterization)
- flaky_tests: [] (EMPTY)
- consistent_failures: [] (EMPTY)
- run_pass_counts: INCONCLUSIVE — the 3x serial sweep did NOT finish in available time. Run 1 was ~70% when output forced; runs 2 and 3 never started. No run produced a completed pass/fail summary or JUnit XML. Earlier apparent completions ("9256 passed", "RUN1 END rc=1") were confirmed PHANTOM background notifications (verified against pgrep + serial.log + xml presence — processes still running, no xml existed). A single serial run over the subset takes ~11-13+ min wall (slow ML tail: retrieval/synthesis/clinical_retrieval/llm doing real CPU embedding/NLI inference; plateaued ~68-70% while confirmed CPU-active, not hung).
- notes highlights:
  - SCOPE/METHOD: ran `pytest tests/unit tests/polaris_graph -p no:cacheprovider -p no:randomly -m 'not slow and not api and not live' --continue-on-collection-errors --junit-xml=runN.xml -rf -q` STRICTLY SERIALLY via a guarded driver. tests/oracle excluded per instructions. Subset ~9455 tests.
  - CONCURRENCY CONTAMINATION: first two runs accidentally overlapped → burst of ~90 early failures (FFFF at 0-2%). Declared ARTIFACTS of parallel execution, NOT real flakiness; contaminated run killed and restarted strictly serial. Suite is not safe to run concurrently with itself.
  - UNRELIABLE BACKGROUND NOTIFICATIONS: environment fired FALSE/future completion events; ground truth taken only from pgrep/serial.log/xml presence.
  - COLLECTION ERRORS 35 (NOT 11): this subset shows 35 collection ImportErrors (audit_bundle 10, api 8, benchmark 8, unit 6, plus audit_ir/anti_sycophancy/test_demo_smoke). Flagged as separate from flaky tests; recent config migration ('migrate 832 os.getenv call sites to central resolve()', 'central defaults registry') a plausible source; recommends investigation. NOTE this 35-vs-11 count is on the subset, not the full baseline.
  - DELIVERABLE STATUS: flaky_tests=[] and consistent_failures=[] returned EMPTY because NO completed multi-run data exists — reporting node-ids now would be fabrication. Correct completion = let driver finish 3 runs, diff three run*.xml: failing-in-some-not-all = flaky; failing-in-all-3 = consistent non-governing failures. Early contaminated failures need re-confirmation under clean serial before classification.
  - No governing tests were quarantined or modified (characterization only).

## policy (files written)
- files_written:
  - docs/review_readiness/flakiness_policy.md
  - docs/flaky_quarantine.txt
  - docs/review_readiness/ci_scanning.md
  - .github/workflows/python_ci.yml
  - pytest.ini
- quarantine_count: 0
- scanning_added (all report-only): pip check (dependency integrity), pip-audit (dependency vuln/CVE), pip-licenses (license scan), detect-secrets (secret scan)
- ci_still_report_only: true

# QUESTION
Does this establish a sound flakiness policy that (a) protects the governing measurement from ever being quarantined, (b) tracks flaky non-governing tests without deleting them, (c) adds dep-hash/license/secret scanning as report-only, and (d) keeps CI report-only (not flipped to required)? Any fabricated scan results or a governing test wrongly quarantined?

Note carefully: the scanning_added list is pip check + pip-audit + pip-licenses + detect-secrets. The 3B intent asked specifically for DEPENDENCY-HASH scanning. Assess whether pip check + pip-audit actually satisfy "dependency-hash" or whether that specific class is still MISSING. Also assess whether returning empty flaky/consistent lists with a quarantine_count of 0 (because characterization never finished) is the HONEST/correct outcome, or a gap that blocks the gate.

End your verdict with exactly one token on its own line: 3B-OK or 3B-REVISE.

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/threeb_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.description: Run codex gate on 3B flakiness policy

content.content:
- (a) No governing test was quarantined, but permanent protection is not proven because the policy file’s contents are not provided.
- (b) Empty flaky/consistent lists and zero quarantine are honest—not fabricated—but the unfinished three-run characterization means flakiness and the stability bar remain unproven.
- (c) License and secret scanning were added report-only. Dependency-hash verification is still missing: `pip check` validates installed-package compatibility, and `pip-audit` checks vulnerabilities; neither verifies cryptographic dependency hashes.
- (d) CI remains report-only, so this requirement passes.
- No fabricated scan results are presented; no scan results are presented at all.
- No governing test was wrongly quarantined.

The incomplete N-run characterization and missing dependency-hash enforcement block the gate.

3B-REVISE
